bug_analysis: The _invoke_field_validators method attempts to subscript the data parameter at line 894 with `data[field_obj.attribute or field_name]`. When deserialize returns None for invalid input types (introduced in PR #1323), this line fails with TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable because data is None but the code assumes it is always a subscriptable object.
exploration_notes: The method is called from _do_load at line 674. The deserialize method in marshalling.py was changed in PR #1323 to return None when input data is not a collections.Mapping, but _invoke_field_validators was not updated to handle this case. PR #1323 changes: before: the code would proceed with processing even for non-Mapping data, after: `if not isinstance(data, collections.Mapping): ... return None`. The buggy code assumes data is always subscriptable but PR #1323 introduced a case where data can be None.

messages.content:
Explore the following repository and produce a localization report identifying where the bug lives.

<instance_id>marshmallow-code__marshmallow-1343</instance_id>

<problem_statement>
[version 2.20.0] TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
After update from version 2.19.5 to 2.20.0 I got error for code like:

```python
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, validates


class Bar(Schema):
    value = fields.String()

    @validates('value')  # <- issue here
    def validate_value(self, value):
        pass


class Foo(Schema):
    bar = fields.Nested(Bar)


sch = Foo()

sch.validate({
    'bar': 'invalid',
})
```

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/_/bug_mschema.py", line 19, in <module>
    'bar': 'invalid',
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 628, in validate
    _, errors = self._do_load(data, many, partial=partial, postprocess=False)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 670, in _do_load
    index_errors=self.opts.index_errors,
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/marshalling.py", line 292, in deserialize
    index=(index if index_errors else None)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/marshalling.py", line 65, in call_and_store
    value = getter_func(data)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/marshalling.py", line 285, in <lambda>
    data
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/fields.py", line 265, in deserialize
    output = self._deserialize(value, attr, data)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/fields.py", line 465, in _deserialize
    data, errors = self.schema.load(value)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 588, in load
    result, errors = self._do_load(data, many, partial=partial, postprocess=True)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 674, in _do_load
    self._invoke_field_validators(unmarshal, data=result, many=many)
  File "/_/env/lib/python3.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 894, in _invoke_field_validators
    value = data[field_obj.attribute or field_name]
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
```

</problem_statement>

<hints>
Thanks for reporting. I was able to reproduce this on 2.20.0. This is likely a regression from https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/pull/1323 . I don't have time to look into it now. Would appreciate a PR.
</hints>

content.text:
<think>
Now I have a clear understanding of the issue:

1. PR #1323 changed the behavior in `marshalling.py`'s `deserialize` method
2. When the input `data` is not a `collections.Mapping`, it now returns `None` instead of proceeding further
3. However, the calling code in `schema.py`'s `_invoke_field_validators` method still expects the result to be subscriptable
4. When validation happens on nested fields with invalid types (like passing a string 'invalid' instead of a dict to a nested field), the deserialize method returns None
5. Then `_invoke_field_validators` tries to access `data[field_obj.attribute or field_name]` where `data` is None, causing the TypeError

The bug is in the `_invoke_field_validators` method in schema.py at line 894. It doesn't handle the case where `data` could be None (which can happen when deserialize returns None for invalid input types).

Let me provide the JSON now.
</think>

```json
{
  "relevant_files": ["/testbed/src/marshmallow/schema.py"],
  "relevant_symbols": ["Schema._invoke_field_validators"],
  "bug_analysis": "The _invoke_field_validators method attempts to subscript the data parameter at line 894 with `data[field_obj.attribute or field_name]`. When deserialize returns None for invalid input types (introduced in PR #1323), this line fails with TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable because data is None but the code assumes it is always a subscriptable object.",
  "exploration_notes": "The method is called from _do_load at line 674. The deserialize method in marshalling.py was changed in PR #1323 to return None when input data is not a collections.Mapping, but _invoke_field_validators was not updated to handle this case. PR #1323 changes: before: the code would proceed with processing even for non-Mapping data, after: `if not isinstance(data, collections.Mapping): ... return None`. The buggy code assumes data is always subscriptable but PR #1323 introduced a case where data can be None."
}
```
